title: Recover Missing HTML
originalDescription: Open Bloom, select a book, close Bloom, delete the .htm file (NOT the bookhtml.bak file), and restart Bloom. Bloom should recover and display a warning to the user. Message says: "Bloom had a problem reading this book and recovered by restoring a recent backup. Please check recent changes to this book. If this happens for no obvious reason, please report it to us."

originalDescription: With a fresh machine (VM or uninstall Bloom and erase all vestiges of Bloom: go to C:/Users/<name>/AppData/Local/SIL/Bloom.  Remove all directories that start with 5.X.  There may be other directories as well in that area that aren't so obvious named something like ~/AppData/Local/SIL/Bloom.exe_Url_<guid?>.):  Register for an earlier version of Bloom, then upgrade Bloom. Ensure your registration info has been saved.

title: task uses some special test code
originalDescription:
This task uses some special test code in Bloom that is activated by editing one of the books in a special collection, but only if you are in a Debug build or of the environment variable BloomSandbox is set to yes. So make sure one of those is true. May not work in a release build even with BloomSandbox set.
Unzip the "Fake Error Report Collection" (See Bloom Team/Testing/Bloompacks). Open the books one by one. Verify error messages according to screenshots. [N.B. willing to live with BL-12226.]
description:
This task uses some special test code in Bloom that is activated by editing one of the books in a special collection, but only if you are in a Debug build or of the environment variable BloomSandbox is set to yes. So make sure one of those is true. May not work in a release build even with BloomSandbox set.
Unzip the "Fake Error Report Collection" (See Bloom Team/Testing/Bloompacks). Open the books one by one. Verify error messages according to screenshots. [N.B. willing to live with BL-12226.]
stepDescription: Enable BloomSandbox test code, open sample books, verify errors.
checklistSteps: Use a Debug build, or set the BloomSandbox environment variable to yes.
Unzip the Fake Error Report Collection from Bloom Team/Testing/Bloompacks.
Open the books one by one.
stepNotes: A release build may still not work even with BloomSandbox set.
Verify the error messages match the screenshots.
BL-12226 is a known issue the team is willing to live with.
bodyChecklistItems: Use a Debug build, or set the BloomSandbox environment variable to yes.
Unzip the Fake Error Report Collection from Bloom Team/Testing/Bloompacks.
Open the books one by one.

title: Badges Book thumbnail badges indicating Bloom
originalDescription: Badges: Book thumbnail badges indicating Bloom Library status. There are four statuses seen here. 1) uploaded & harvested 2) waiting for harvester 3) uploaded as draft 4) uploaded and removed from publication. Completion of harvesting -- movement from State (2) to (1) or (2) to 3) -- will only be shown after re-booting Bloom. Likewise, state (4) is only detected after initial re-boot (or changing collections). Test bulk upload.

title: Update Books without losing branding make
originalDescription: Update Books without losing branding: make a book with any current project branding and upload it then download it for editing. A new instance of Bloom opens. Go to settings Enterprise and change the branding to None. Upload to web again. This time the "yes" option should require you to tick a box to acknowledge the branding is changing to None. Tick both and upload. Verify branding for the book on Blorg. Change branding to None and reupload. Verify you have to acknowledge the change in branding. Upload and verify result on Blorg.

title: Changing ownership Log as Moderator download
originalDescription: Changing ownership: Log in as a Moderator and download a book for editing from the Pratham bookshelf which is not owned by either Moderator, Collection Editor, or Regular user. After Bloom opens, go to Publish to Web, tick agreements, and click upload. Under the "yes" option, the collision dialog should present tick box to change ownership. Don't upload. Close Bloom and log out as moderator and log in a Collection Editor. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should allow you to change ownership. Don't upload. Close Bloom and log out as Collection Editor and log in a Regular user. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should not present the option to change ownership and should not allow you to upload (i.e. after clicking "yes", the "upload" button should remain greyed out). Close Bloom and log out as Regular user and log in a Collection Editor. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should allow you to change ownership. Tick boxes and upload. Verify on Blorg that ownership has changed. Try re-uploading. This time the change-ownership box won't appear because owership has been transferred.

title: Choose ONE particular branding examine following
originalDescription:
Choose ONE particular branding and examine the following:

- Check logos appear correctly in the 1) Edit view 2) PDF 3) EPUB 4) Bloom Reader.  See the "Branding Screenshots" sheet in this file. [See settings/Bloom Enterprise. See the folder browser/branding/X for hints on what should appear for each branding. Images should appear where their names suggest. Json settings should be applied to new books.]  The brandings should be checked in generated books, not just in the Bloom preview. See notes at https://docs.google.com/document/d/1IRwsfHnvmR0AnhiJFgp8EOrFAhXmF1U3tOGe-Y7p2oA/edit?usp=sharing. Remember to set the appropriate xmatter, if the branding has a custom one associated with it. (JT 6.3: as far as I can find in the subscriptions doc, there is no longer a current Kyrgyz branding. This makes it difficult to test all the things in the notes. I tried the Little Zebra branding, which has obvious custom stuff on the bottom of the front cover, the bottom of the title page, and the top of the outside back cover. It does not, however, have hyperlinks.)

title: Create book special unique font which
originalDescription: Create a book with a special/unique font which Harvester knows about.  The Harvester should succeed, and the book should be able to be read online with that font.  Downloaded ePUB and BloomPub books should contain the font so that they can be read with it.  If the font was used in a style, then the style in a downloaded book should still refer to the font even if hasn't been installed.  [Noto Sans Tamil from Google fonts is one that Harvester knows about.  Scheherazade New from SIL is another such font.]

title: Verify book thumbnail Library is just
originalDescription: Verify that the book thumbnail on Library is just the image of the front cover. [JT: I think it's sufficient to upload a book (to our 'sandbox', dev.bloomlibrary.org), follow the link to the resulting book, and check that the thumbnail just look like the front cover picture. For example, it doesn't have the title (not on  inside the rectangle that has the cove-color background, anyway or any of the other text, and the size and shape match the picture rather than the whole cover.]

title: Text Languages Ensure 1 all content
originalDescription: Text Languages: Ensure 1) all content languages are presented and your selection is remembered if you exit publish or switch between BloomPUB and Web Publish; 2) checkboxes are disabled and checked for any "required" languages regardless of completeness (e.g. a trilingual book will have three checked languages which are greyed out); 3) only the languages selected are uploaded (web) or embedded in saved BloomPUB; 4) tooltip says "Select this if you want readers to be able to choose to read the book in this language"; 5) if the book is bilingual or trilingual, then those languages cannot be unchecked regardless of completeness. 6) incomplete languages are correctly identified ; 7) a language which is only in xmatter is not advertised on blorg [Quick test resource: download this book in an English collection and publish. There should be English, French, Hausa, Spanish & Swahili.]

title: Talking Book Languages Ensure 1 all
originalDescription:
Talking Book Languages: Ensure 1) all audio languages embedded in the book are presented and are checked by default and your selection is remembered if you exit publish or switch between BloomPUB and Web Publish; 2) all boxes can be unchecked and; 3) tooltip says "Select this if you want to include this audio"; 4) the checkmark beside Features: Talking Book is checked iff there is one audio language checked. [Quick test: download this book into an English collection and publish. There should audio in English & Tok Pisin.] 5) Talking Book Language X is advertised/listed under Talking Book Languages if and only if: there exists some text in language X in at least one content/interior page of the book
6) Talking Book Language X is included in a BloomPUB if and only if:  language X is selected in the list of Talking Book Languages.

originalDescription: Run several tests to ensure that when re-uploading books to Bloom Library, only files that have changed are uploaded. title: Create book Enterprise only features Turn
originalDescription: Create a book with Enterprise-only features. Turn off Enterprise. Publishing refuses to open, with a warning message to users.  With Enterprise turned off, you can publish a derivative book with overlays but cannot publish a book with any added overlays (Bloom will show a screen saying Enterprise Required and prompting you to active Enterprise or remove the Overlays).  Other Enterprise pages may allow publishing, but Game pages (at least) are stripped from uploading and BloomPUB creation.  Note that both ePUB and Audio/Video publishing require a Pro subscription regardless of content.

Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.

title: Reupload by user Moderator permission Bloom
originalDescription:

After clicking the Upload Book button, the collision shows you have permission. Ensure the uploader avatar matches the original uploader which is different from the avatar beside "you have permission". Under the "yes" option is a tick box to change the uploader. Do not tick that box. Choose "Yes" and then the UPLOAD button. Verify the uploaded book has the new title and has retained the original uploader.
Verify that Draft status is the same.

originalDescription:
Bulk Upload Quick Test
- Copy "Bulk Upload Quick Test" folder from Testing\Materials for Bloom Editor Testing\Bulk Upload Quick Test into Document/Bloom and open it
- update the Bloom Subscription to a newer valid one if necessary; make Bloom Library Bookshelf to be none; delete any of the books if they are still there from the last test pass
- Choose any book, Publish to Web, choose Upload this collection --> warning about needing to set the bookshelf
- set bookshelf and return to upload collection
- confirm results of 4 new books uploaded.
- On Blorg, confirm each according to the screenshots included in the front covers
- Without changing anything, republish collection: -> 4 books skipped
- Change one book, republish --> 1 book updated, 3 books skipped

title: We now only upload files have

Test a couple of different ways: changed audio or video, changed image, deleted pages, etc.

title: Test all aspects upload download ensure
originalDescription: - Test all aspects of upload and download to ensure all files are correctly transmitted. This should not affect things like features or languages. After uploading a changed book, download it and inspect it. (JT 6.3: not sure what is meant by not affecting things like features or languages. Presumably if these changed in the updated book, they should change on Blorg, too, after re-uploading. "All aspects" is very broad...that's why we have many lines of test activities for upload. I took it to mean each kind of page element...images, video, widgets.)

title: Dragging corner controls resizes bubble try
originalDescription: Dragging corner controls resizes bubble: try all bubble types, all corners; try at multiple zoom levels. Most bubbles, including picture buttons, can be resized to any shape; but image and video overlays can only be sized in a way that preserves the aspect ratio of the picture. Again, things should snap to a grid unless ctrl is held down, and red alignment lines should show as appropriate; also green bars when the item being resized is the same height as something else. (Text bubbles are special and do not have corner handles.)

This should be indicated by the message at the bottom of the character tab: "This formatting is for all LANGUAGE text boxes with "XXX" style". But see https://issues.bloomlibrary.org/youtrack/issue/BL-9965 before writing up inheritence issues.

title: Copy Paste scripture text Paratext project
originalDescription:
Copy/Paste scripture text from a Paratext project into a Bloom book to ensure that a) the text is not duplicated; b) superscripts for verse numbers are handled reasonably.
Bloom has code to find "\v N" and convert it to superscript, but this doesn't work when the incoming text is in html because of the markup. In my 6.1-6.3 testing w/PTX 9.5, pasting with Ctrl-V pasted as html and therefore did not give superscripts. Ctrl-Shift-V pasted as text and did.
If superscripts are added, those should be reversible using the standard ckeditor control.

Originally this said: superscripts for verse numbers are formatted as such when copying from "Unformatted" view; otherwise, from "Preview" & "Standard" you get unformatted numbers for verses, and "Formatted" gives backslash codes. Note, "HTML clipboard" prefix is ok.

- Books from other Collections (added via "Show another collection..." button) : Show in File Explorer; Move into {current collection}

title: Test right click rename function rename
originalDescription: - Test the right-click rename function. This should rename the book's folder and .htm file, not the title of the book. Can also be started using F2. Subsequent renamings of the title should not affect the renamed folder -- it should be "locked". Right-click rename delete should restore the book back to match the title and unlock the book, i.e. renaming the title should not affect the book folder name. Ensure the position of the book thumbnail does not shift when renaming. Try renaming a book with a html5 widget (cf. BL-12598). If you make a BloomPub or other publications, the default filename should be based on the folder name, not the title. (JT 6.3: but if you made e.g. a BloomPub previously, Bloom remembers the name you used to make it, and that is the default rather than the current folder name. This is probably intentional, or at least OK, so I'm not making a bug report.)

originalDescription: BloomPUB tab: Make a new book. Check Book Settings: BloomPUB. The setting should be set to 1280x720. Close settings. From Bloom Team\Testing\Materials for Bloom Editor Testing\Images\4K Images, add "1 Samuel.jpg" and "2 Dylan.jpg" to the book and then publish the book to BlooomPUB with a variety of Image Resolutions and save to disk. Unzip the saved BloomPUBs and verify the size of the images matches this screenshot. Upload to blorg (use draft), wait for Harvesting, download the BloomPUB artifact and confirm that images there follow the same file sizes.

title: installed French DR LR source make
originalDescription:
- From installed French DR/LR source, make a derivative of "French DR". Mimimal Test procedure:
  1) Progressively move from page to page, observe and verify. For pages 1-13, each page should contain no highlighted words if the corresponding Stage is set, e.g. page 5 will show no highlighted words if stage is set to 5. Exceptions: Page 14 requires stage 11, & page 15 requires stage 12.
‌  2) Try adding 2 or 3 sight words to a stage which would not otherwise be acceptable for that stage, and ensure these words are not highlighted if added to a page.
  3) Remove diagraph "ch" from Letters, then on page 1 add the word "cha". Set stage to 1. "cha" should not be highlighted.

title: Download one Look Listen Live books
originalDescription: Download one of the Look, Listen, and Live books which has long stretches of audio. In Edit, play the audio, then stop the audio. Then resume the audio. Add additional segment markders (|) to cut up the text, using both the keyboard and the Advanced:"Insert Segment Marker". Run Adjust Timings again. Click Play. It should start from the beginning. It should highlight according to your segment markers. (JT: when we fix BL-15678, we should note expectations here regarding previous adjustments to other segments. Will this reset all segments to their default positions, or keep any manual adjustments the user made to pre-existing breaks?)

title: Edit add new Quiz page Ensure
originalDescription:

- Ensure the Quiz answer "cells" don't spill into the page number on a 16x9 Landscape or Portrait page, or a A5 page. Note: Decision in BL-9952 is we don't report overflow on Quiz pages. 
- Note also: we assume the user manages his/her text to not overflow (which is currently possible if you have multiple lines in the Question field). 
- A future improvement might allow scrolling,  We just haven't got around to it (JH).

originalDescription: [5.6] Error during installation when machine is in Dutch (JT 6.3: used https://xupefei.github.io/Locale-Emulator/, set app profile for installer exe to Dutch and told it to fake system UI language. Installer ran normally, but did not get the expected UI asking which localization to use. Tried using it on BloomBetaInternal.exe, but no context menu item: Locale Emulator does not handle 64-bit exes. Tried Locale Remulator, which does. Fiddled with LFConfig.xml to add Dutch, since its UI for configuration does not work. Tried running Bloom supposedly set to Dutch. No crash, but no UI asking me to choose locale. Tried built-in Run in Japanese. Same result. Don't think this is worth more effort, so checking off.)
